commit.message:
fix: make phive failure fatal + skip vendor classes in mock rule

PhiveUpdatePlugin now throws RuntimeException when tool-install.bash
fails, instead of silently swallowing the error. QA tools (PHPStan,
PHP-CS-Fixer, Infection) won't work without PHARs — this must be fatal.

ForbidMockingFinalClassRule now skips third-party final classes (those
in vendor/). Only project-internal final classes are flagged, since
those are the ones you can add interfaces to. Third-party finals like
Symfony's Security class are outside your control.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(ui-server): fix double-nesting signal collection in Fast Refresh [#721] (#928)

The factory wrapper and __$refreshPerform both called
startSignalCollection/stopSignalCollection. Since signal collection
uses a stack, the wrapper's inner collector captured all signals,
then __$refreshTrack discarded them during refresh mode. This left
__$refreshPerform with an empty signal array, so signal state was
never preserved across HMR cycles.

Fix: add a refreshSignals stash in __$refreshTrack so the wrapper's
collected signals are passed back to __$refreshPerform. Remove the
redundant outer signal collection from __$refreshPerform.

Co-authored-by: vertz-dev-front[bot] <2828126+vertz-dev-front[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add Organon philosophical reasoning engine plugin

Turns 62 principles from 20 philosophers into a live decision engine
and code review framework for Claude Code. Based on the Organon project
by Lightcyphers SRL (CC BY-SA 4.0).

Features:
- Auto-detect mode: reads context, selects decision or review mode
- Decision engine: situation analysis with principle routing and protocol
- Philosophical review: 12-dimension code quality assessment
- Three depth levels: Quick, Standard, Deep (full 22-step protocol)
- Philosopher-council agent for deep analysis via Opus
- 15 reference files with complete organon content (zero loss)
- 3 commands: /organon, /organon:decide, /organon:review

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Use intuitive full-size parameters for box/cylinder/prism primitives

Chisel now matches Fogleman's convention: box(1.5) means a 1.5×1.5×1.5
box, not half-extent 1.5 (which would be 3×3×3). The codegen divides
by 2 internally when passing to GLSL sdBox/sdCylinder/etc.

Affected primitives (size/height params halved in codegen):
- box(s) and box(w,h,d) — size is full size
- rounded_box, box_frame — same
- cylinder(r, h) — h is full height (not half-height)
- hex_prism, tri_prism, octagon_prism — h is full height
- capped_cone(h, r1, r2) — h is full height

Unchanged (already intuitive):
- sphere, torus, capsule, octahedron, pyramid, ellipsoid, cone,
  round_cone, solid_angle, rhombus, horseshoe, capped_torus
- Endpoint-based variants (no halving needed)

All shader files updated to use new convention (values doubled
to preserve visual output).

Add MCP (Model Context Protocol) support for external tool servers

LLMs can now use tools from external MCP servers (filesystem, browser,
databases, etc.) alongside native tools. MCP servers are configured in
Settings UI and tools are auto-discovered and injected into prompts.

Backend: rmcp crate, McpManager in worker process, stdio transport,
tool namespacing (mcp__server__tool), DB persistence, 6 API endpoints.
Frontend: McpSettingsSection component, useMcpServers hook.

Fix Load DMA stolen cycles: use cpuCycleCount parity for GET/PUT phase

Load DMA stolen cycle count now uses Master Clock derived cpuCycleCount
parity instead of cpuBusIsWrite proxy, correctly handling reads that land
on PUT cycles (e.g. AccuracyCoin clockslide sequences).

Reload DMA retains cpuBusIsWrite proxy (empirically correct for blargg).

174/174 blargg tests PASS. AccuracyCoin 76/81 PASS (remaining 5 are
unstable illegal opcodes requiring sub-cycle DMC timing precision).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add rollback checkpoint system

- POST /api/rollback { ref } — writes git tag/hash to .pin then exits;
  loop does git fetch + checkout that ref on restart
- POST /api/restart — now also clears .pin so loop returns to latest
- ./run --dev loop checks for .pin before each restart; falls back to git pull
- .gitignore: exclude cache/ and .pin from commits

Deploy workflow:
  git push && curl -X POST http://192.168.100.1:4000/api/restart

Rollback workflow:
  curl -X POST http://192.168.100.1:4000/api/rollback -H 'content-type: application/json' -d '{"ref":"checkpoint-name"}'

commit.message:
feat: add software engineering handbook

Complete workflow system for managing 25+ repositories with AI agents.

Documents:
- Project initialization (templates, structure)
- Feature development workflow (issue → release)
- Issue management (templates, labels, lifecycle)
- Pull request process (template, checklist, merge)
- Code review guidelines (checklist, feedback)
- Release process (semantic versioning, deployment)
- Documentation standards (README, code docs)
- AI agent integration (project context, prompts)
- Repository templates

Principle: Human-driven, AI-executed - humans decide WHAT,
AI does HOW. Consistency across all repositories through
standardized templates and rules.

fix: Expand John Pyle confirmed URL table with high-quality St Catharine's portraits

Problem: John Pyle portrait was not resembling the correct person (Cambridge atmospheric
chemist FRS). Only 2 reference images were available (Royal Society 550×550 + Cambridge
thumbnail 150×200), and they were insufficient for accurate likeness generation.

Solution:
- Added St Catharine's College Nathan Pitt 2015 portrait (1896×1422, 682KB) — primary
- Added St Catharine's 2015 directory portrait (631×632, 103KB) — secondary
- Added Cambridge Chemistry original jap12.jpg (399×531, 160KB) — better than 150×200 thumbnail
- Added Cambridge Symposium 2024 cover photo (1200×1200, 210KB) — most recent
- Maintained Royal Society photo (550×550)

Now 5 confirmed URLs sorted by quality, all HTTP 200 verified.
The 1896×1422 Nathan Pitt professional portrait is the highest-quality reference
yet found — should significantly improve likeness accuracy.

repository.description: Deterministic CLI tool for managing development tasks - build your backlog during the day, let AI finish it overnight

commit.message:
Rewrite skill.md: watcher + agent decision architecture

The watcher script now only listens for events and exits on your_turn
or game_over. The agent itself analyzes hands and submits actions via
curl, enabling LLM-powered strategic thinking instead of hardcoded
decision logic. Also adds 401 error handling to prevent retry loops.

fix(backend): 6 bugs blocking live LLM integration

- Fix invalid model IDs: claude-3-5-haiku → claude-haiku-4-5-20251001
  in intent_classifier, base agent, and migration seed data
- Fix raw SQL in PostgREST .gte() filters: use Python datetime instead
  of `now() - interval` which isn't valid as a parameter value
- Fix UUID serialization in SubAgent execution logging: use
  model_dump(mode="json") for JSON-safe output
- Fix copilot response formatting: convert structured dict responses
  to readable markdown instead of Python repr strings

Tested all 6 layers: gateway → agents → orchestrator → API → UI.
All passing with real Anthropic API calls.

fix: deep recursive search for PDF in tool results

recursive search. Finds pdfBase64+filename at any nesting level,

step structure when PDF is not found for debugging.

commit.message:
Fix auth baseURL for production

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add Memory Agent — autonomous knowledge custodian with 6 self-healing passes

New Celery periodic task (default 6h) that autonomously maintains the knowledge
corpus: duplicate detection with LLM-synthesized merge and fallback, orphan node
cleanup, deep contradiction scanning across domains, backlink reinforcement for
pre-feature memories, confidence decay on stale unconfirmed memories, and cluster
coherence with domain reclassification.

Includes Redis SETNX lock guard, per-pass error isolation, configurable batch
limits, and webhook reporting for all 6 event types. Also fixes Docker networking
for Ollama container access from API/worker containers.

424 tests pass (18 new).

Add error handling to db.Create() and ShouldBindJSON() calls in backend

- Add error checks to 10 db.Create() calls that silently swallowed errors:
  CreateRideShare, JoinRideShare, CreateDelivery, CreateOrder,
  AddSavedAddress, AddPaymentMethod, CreateStore, CreatePromo,
  SendChatMessage, GetWalletBalance

  SetAvailability, UpdateDriverProfile, UpdateStore, UpdatePromo
- Add nil pointer guard for Driver.User in GetAvailableRideShares

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add real-time feature display and unified validation

New features:
- `make show`: Real-time feature display streaming to terminal
  - Shows price, spread, trade activity, order flow, tick entropy
  - Updates every second with regime interpretation
  - No file output, purely for monitoring
  - Usage: `make show SYMBOL=ETH`

- `make validate`: Now runs ALL validations sequentially
  - API connection validation
  - Position tracking validation
  - Whale identification validation
  - Tick entropy validation
  - Reports GO/NO-GO for each test

- Improved Makefile with organized sections and better help

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Replace line selector with continuous tap/drag bar

Server:
- line category marked continuous:true, no longer uses discrete vote counters
- linePositions Map per sheet stores float 0-1 per voter
- new vote-line socket event and broadcastLineUpdate function
- endVoting computes line winner as average of all positions (float 0-1)
- publicSheet exposes lineAvg and lineCount
- join-sheet/disconnect clean up linePositions correctly
- start-round clears linePositions

Voter (phone):
- Line category replaced with a horizontal tap/drag bar (2' to 12')
- Drag or tap anywhere; gold dot marker follows finger
- Other voters' positions shown as blue dots above the bar
- Average of all taps shown as semi-transparent vertical marker
- Pick summary shows result as feet+inches (e.g. "7' 4"")

Display (TV):
- Line vote bars replaced with a distribution bar visualization
- Blue dots at each voter position, gold average marker
- foot labels (2' through 12') along bar
- Result screen shows line as feet+inches via formatLineResult()

commit.message:
docs: add 5 enforcement hook patterns to common-solutions (#91-95) (#141)

New patterns from Claude Code enforcement hooks implementation:
- #91: Hook exit codes — only 0 (allow) and 2 (deny), no native "ask"
- #92: Fast-path whitelist for PreToolUse hooks (<10ms safe commands)
- #93: Deterministic hooks replace LLM-dependent instructions
- #94: Session flag files for cross-hook communication
- #95: Safety = hard block, Quality = soft remind philosophy
